title: polypodium subnormale
intro: Polypodium subnormale is a species of fern, specifically a type of Polypodiaceae family, commonly found in various parts of the world, particularly in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which make it useful in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Polypodium subnormale include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
